name: European Convention on Human Rights (ECHR / CETS 005)
summary: The European Convention on Human Rights (ECHR / formally Convention for the Protection of Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms CETS 005) is a European supranational meta-framework human-rights binding-treaty instrument adopted by the Council of Europe — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework with formal individual-applications jurisdiction (ECHR adopted 4 November 1950 + effective 3 September 1953 — formal European regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive human-rights framework + European Court of Human Rights ECtHR formal judicial-body framework with formal individual-applications jurisdiction, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks which operate without formal regional-court-individual-applications-jurisdiction framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional human-rights framework with formal regional-court-individual-applications-jurisdiction framework processing ~50,000+ applications per year), as the central jurisdiction of the most distinctive modern Strasbourg Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework (ECHR Article 8 framework on right to respect for private and family life — formal framework underlying European parental-rights + child-protection + parental-alienation case-law framework, structurally distinctive globally as the most-developed modern regional human-rights framework on parental-rights + child-protection framework via Article 8 case-law framework including landmark cases Görgülü v. Germany 26 February 2004 + Maumousseau and Washington v. France 6 December 2007 + Neulinger and Shuruk v. Switzerland 6 July 2010 + Kupiec v. Poland 30 December 2010 + multiple subsequent ECtHR cases applying Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework to parental-alienation framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal European post-WWII human-rights elaboration framework (ECHR adopted as European regional foundational human-rights binding-treaty framework — applicable to all 46 Council of Europe member states as of 2026 with universal European adoption framework, structurally distinctive globally as the most-widely-adopted regional human-rights binding-treaty framework with formal individual-applications-jurisdiction framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the European Court of Human Rights ECtHR framework with formal Grand Chamber + Chamber + Committee + Judge frameworks (ECtHR established 21 January 1959 under ECHR Article 19 framework + reformed via Protocol No. 11 1998 + Protocol No. 14 2010 + Protocol No. 15 2021 — formal European regional court framework with formal pilot-judgment + interim-measures + advisory-opinion frameworks, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks operating without formal regional-court framework). The ECHR is binding upon all 46 Council of Europe member states as of 2026 (excluding Russian Federation post-16 March 2022 expulsion + Belarus + Holy See never-joined frameworks). The Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Article 8 framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework. The European Court of Human Rights is the apex judicial body framework with formal individual-applications jurisdiction. The ECHR is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: European Convention on Human Rights
statutory_framework.relevance: Convention for the Protection of Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms CETS 005 adopted 4 November 1950 + effective 3 September 1953 — formal European regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive human-rights framework.

statutory_framework.citation: ECHR Article 8 (Right to Respect for Private and Family Life)
statutory_framework.title: ECHR Article 8
statutory_framework.relevance: ECHR Article 8 framework on right to respect for private and family life — formal framework underlying European parental-rights + child-protection + parental-alienation case-law framework.

statutory_framework.citation: European Court of Human Rights ECtHR Establishment 1959 (ECHR Article 19)
statutory_framework.title: ECtHR Establishment
statutory_framework.relevance: European Court of Human Rights ECtHR established 21 January 1959 under ECHR Article 19 framework — formal European regional court framework with formal individual-applications jurisdiction.

statutory_framework.title: ECHR Protocol No. 11
statutory_framework.relevance: ECHR Protocol No. 11 effective 1 November 1998 — formal reform of ECtHR framework establishing permanent court framework + formal individual-applications jurisdiction expansion.

statutory_framework.citation: ECtHR Article 8 Positive Obligations Doctrine Case-Law Framework (Görgülü 2004 + Neulinger 2010 + others)
statutory_framework.title: ECtHR Article 8 Positive Obligations Doctrine
statutory_framework.relevance: ECtHR Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework via landmark cases Görgülü v. Germany 26 February 2004 + Maumousseau and Washington v. France 6 December 2007 + Neulinger and Shuruk v. Switzerland 6 July 2010 + Kupiec v. Poland 30 December 2010 + multiple subsequent cases — formal European parental-alienation case-law framework.

apex_courts.name: European Court of Human Rights (ECtHR)
apex_courts.role: Apex judicial body framework with formal individual-applications jurisdiction for ECHR violations + ECHR Grand Chamber + Chamber + Committee + Judge frameworks.

apex_courts.name: Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe
apex_courts.role: Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe — formal supervisory framework for ECtHR judgment execution.

anonymisation_convention: ECHR-related individual communications are anonymised per ECtHR practice using initials where appropriate.

key_developments.title: Council of Europe founded
key_developments.description: Council of Europe founded 5 May 1949 — beginning of formal Council of Europe regional framework.

key_developments.title: ECHR adopted
key_developments.description: Convention for the Protection of Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms CETS 005 adopted 4 November 1950 — formal European regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive human-rights framework.

key_developments.title: ECHR enters force
key_developments.description: Convention for the Protection of Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms entered force 3 September 1953 — formal European regional binding-treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: ECtHR established
key_developments.description: European Court of Human Rights ECtHR established 21 January 1959 under ECHR Article 19 framework — formal European regional court framework.

key_developments.title: ECHR Protocol No. 11 ECtHR Reform
key_developments.description: ECHR Protocol No. 11 effective 1 November 1998 — formal reform of ECtHR framework establishing permanent court framework.

key_developments.title: Görgülü v. Germany
key_developments.description: ECtHR Görgülü v. Germany Judgment 26 February 2004 — landmark Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework on parental-rights + child-protection framework.

key_developments.title: Neulinger and Shuruk v. Switzerland
key_developments.description: ECtHR Neulinger and Shuruk v. Switzerland Grand Chamber Judgment 6 July 2010 — landmark Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework on cross-border-child-abduction + parental-alienation framework.

key_developments.title: Russian Federation expulsion
key_developments.description: Russian Federation expelled from Council of Europe 16 March 2022 + ceased to be ECHR state party 16 September 2022 — formal end of Russian Federation ECHR framework.

structural_findings: ECHR operates a supranational European regional human-rights binding-treaty framework — places ECHR in the European supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework with formal individual-applications jurisdiction is structurally distinctive globally — formal European Court of Human Rights ECtHR framework processing ~50,000+ applications per year.

Central jurisdiction of most distinctive modern Strasbourg Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal framework underlying European parental-rights + child-protection + parental-alienation case-law framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal European post-WWII human-rights elaboration framework is structurally distinctive globally — most-widely-adopted regional human-rights binding-treaty framework with formal individual-applications-jurisdiction framework.

Central jurisdiction of European Court of Human Rights ECtHR framework with formal Grand Chamber + Chamber + Committee + Judge frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — formal regional court framework with formal pilot-judgment + interim-measures + advisory-opinion frameworks.

All 46 Council of Europe member state ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — universal European adoption framework, distinct from Istanbul Convention 2011 38-state-party + Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children 2003 15-state-party frameworks.
Russian Federation expulsion 16 March 2022 framework is structurally distinctive globally — first formal expulsion from Council of Europe in Council of Europe history.
Görgülü 2004 + Neulinger 2010 + multiple subsequent Article 8 cases parental-alienation-case-law-framework is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: ECHR jurisdiction sidecar — supranational European regional human-rights binding-treaty meta-framework (ECHR 1950/1953 + ECHR Article 8 + ECtHR Establishment 1959 + ECHR Protocol No. 11 1998 + Görgülü 2004 + Neulinger 2010 + Council of Europe 1949 + Russian Federation expulsion 2022). Only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework with formal individual-applications jurisdiction globally + central jurisdiction of most distinctive modern Strasbourg Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework + central jurisdiction of formal European post-WWII human-rights elaboration framework + central jurisdiction of European Court of Human Rights ECtHR framework + All 46 Council of Europe member state ratification framework universal European adoption + Russian Federation expulsion 16 March 2022 first formal expulsion from Council of Europe + Görgülü 2004 + Neulinger 2010 + multiple subsequent Article 8 cases parental-alienation-case-law-framework. Council of Europe regional-children-and-parental-rights triad complete (ECHR 1950 + Council of Europe Convention on the Exercise of Children's Rights 1996 + Council of Europe Convention on Contact concerning Children 2003).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: ECtHR Article 8 positive-obligations doctrine framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework, though no formal ECtHR judgment formally applying Article 8 to parental-alienation framework as such.

Joins European + supranational + regional-human-rights-binding-treaty cluster + ECHR-only-regional-binding-treaty-with-formal-court-individual-applications-jurisdiction-globally-distinctive + Article-8-positive-obligations-doctrine-Strasbourg-PA-case-law + Görgülü-2004 + Neulinger-2010 + Kupiec-2010 + Maumousseau-Washington-2007 + ECtHR-50000-plus-applications-per-year + universal-46-Council-of-Europe-state-party + Russian-Federation-expulsion-2022 + Council-of-Europe-Convention-2003-Contact-concerning-Children-complementary + Council-of-Europe-Convention-1996-Exercise-of-Children-Rights-complementary clusters within the corpus.
